stem: Convert the point \( ([[x]],[[y]]) \) to polar form: find \( r \) (with \( r\ge 0 \)).
Convert the point \( ([[x0]],[[x0]]\sqrt{3}) \) to polar form: find \( \theta \), with \( -\pi<\theta\le\pi \).
solution: Since \( \tan\theta=\dfrac{[[x0]]\sqrt{3}}{[[x0]]}=\sqrt{3} \) and the point is in the first quadrant, \( \theta=\dfrac{\pi}{3}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( \tan\theta=\frac{1}{\sqrt3}\) (the angle whose tangent is the reciprocal) instead of \( \sqrt3\).
This mistakes the ratio for an undefined tangent (as if \( x=0\)).
This uses the reference angle's supplement instead of the angle itself.
This gives the correct reference angle but in the wrong (negative) quadrant.
This is off by a small additive amount from the correct angle.
This is off by a small additive amount (other direction) from the correct angle.

stem: A point has polar coordinates \( \left(r,\theta\right)=\left([[r]],\dfrac{\pi}{[[n]]}\right) \). Find its rectangular \( x \)-coordinate.
distractor_rationales: This uses \( \sin\theta\) instead of \( \cos\theta\).
This squares the cosine instead of using it linearly.
This divides by \( \cos\theta\) instead of multiplying.
This doubles the angle before taking the cosine.
This is off by one from the correct \( x\)-coordinate.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct \( x\)-coordinate.

stem: A point in the first quadrant has \( x \)-coordinate \( [[x0]] \) and lies on the ray \( \theta=\dfrac{\pi}{[[n]]} \) from the origin. Find its \( y \)-coordinate.
distractor_rationales: This inverts the tangent ratio.
This uses \( \sin\theta\) instead of \( \tan\theta\).
This squares the tangent instead of using it linearly.
This doubles the angle before taking the tangent.
This is off by one from the correct \( y\)-coordinate.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct \( y\)-coordinate.

stem: The polar equation \( r=2[[a]]\cos\theta+2[[b]]\sin\theta \) describes a circle through the origin. Converting to rectangular form, find the circle's radius.
distractor_rationales: This adds \( a\) and \( b\) directly instead of combining them under a square root.
This subtracts \( b^2\) from \( a^2\) instead of adding.
This reports the radius squared instead of the radius itself.
This doubles both terms under the root unnecessarily.
This is off by one from the correct radius.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct radius.

stem: The vertical line \( x=[[k]] \) has polar equation \( r\cos\theta=[[k]] \). Find \( r \) when \( \theta=\dfrac{\pi}{[[n]]} \).
distractor_rationales: This multiplies by \( \cos\theta\) instead of dividing.
This divides by \( \sin\theta\) instead of \( \cos\theta\).
This divides by the square of the cosine instead of the cosine itself.
This doubles the angle before dividing.
This is off by one from the correct value of \( r\).
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct value of \( r\).
